Intro block

Replace the intro paragraph. Keep "Hi Reader" as-is (HubSpot personalization token).

Subject line

Proxuma Release 26 & 27: See who's available, control who sees what, and a brand new Knowledge Base

Preview text

Resource availability directly on planning screens, reworked permissions, capacity improvements, and the Proxuma Knowledge Base is live.

Body

Welcome to Release 26 & 27 of Proxuma! These two releases bring a set of updates focused on planning visibility and access control. You can now see resource availability exceptions directly in the calendar, a reworked permissions system gives admins fine-grained control over who sees what, and the new Compact Capacity View makes planning for large teams practical. Service calls now support non-working hours deduction, meeting invitation responses show up in event details, and capacity bars got a visual upgrade with color-coded holidays. On top of that, the Proxuma Knowledge Base is live.

Watch the recap or read on to see what's new in this release.

New features: Release 26

Compact Capacity View

A new Compact Capacity View on the Resource Planning screen lets you see more resources and time units at once. If you've been switching to Excel for large-team planning or longer horizons, this is the alternative. Toggle it on and you get a denser view without sacrificing performance. A quarterly timeline option has been added to the Year view as well, so you can plan further ahead in one screen.

Reworked Permissions and Access Control

You now have full control over who can see and access what across Proxuma. The new permissions system works on three levels: page-level view permissions control which screens a user can reach, scope-based access determines which companies, projects, and Power BI files they see, and entity-level permissions handle the rest. Scoping can follow Departments, Line of Business, or Manual Selection. Everything is managed from the updated Security Levels interface in tenant Settings.

  • Page-level View permissions (Yes / No) for every application page, controlling both navigation and direct URL access.
  • Scope selection determines which companies, projects, and Power BI files a user can access.
  • Security Levels management now supports the full permissions module including Pages, Scopes, and Advanced Settings.

Click here to learn more about the new license and security level permissions.

New features: Release 27

Resource Availability Exceptions on Planning Screens

When someone has days off or recurring non-working periods, you now see it directly in the calendar. No more scheduling a resource that isn't available. Exceptions appear on Day, Week, Month, and Year views across both Resource Planning and Project Planning. When you create or edit an event, a warning appears if the selected time falls within an exception. Capacity calculations factor in individual exceptions automatically.

Click here to learn more about setting up your resources' daily availability.

Non-Working Hours Deduction for Service Calls

Service Call events can now include non-working time like travel, breaks, or preparation. This time gets automatically deducted from the total duration when calculating resource and event capacity, so availability figures reflect what's actually usable. You specify the non-working hours directly within the Service Call event.

Click here to learn more about service calls.

Meeting Invitation Response Status in Meeting Details

The Meeting Details tab for Service Calls, General Time Requests, and Shifts now shows whether attendees accepted, declined, tentatively accepted, or haven't responded to the linked Outlook appointment. Attendees and assigned resources appear in two separate lists, each with their response status shown with colors and labels. If the "Require Response to Invitation" setting is off, the section points you to the Outlook Step Wizard to enable it.

Proxuma Knowledge Base - Now Available!

The Proxuma Knowledge Base is available to all users directly from the platform. Browse articles by topic or use the search bar to find answers from any page. Content covers everything from getting started and settings configuration to advanced planning features and integrations. Most articles are user-protected, so log in to Proxuma first before accessing the Knowledge Base articles.

UI updates: Release 27

Capacity Bars Update

Capacity bars and Total Hours bars on Resource Planning screens now show percentages alongside hour values (planned, spare, and overbooked), matching what's already shown in the capacity graph. At narrow column widths, values adapt to the available space instead of becoming unreadable.

Holiday and Time-Off Color Coding in Capacity View

Capacity is now color-coded when full days are completely scheduled with time-off entries or holidays. Holidays are displayed in purple, while other time-off entries appear in orange. This makes it immediately clear at a glance which days are blocked and why.

[Screenshot: Capacity view with color-coded time-off and holidays]
